sections.paragraph: or rather dual. עַפְעַפַּיִם only found in const. עַפְעַפֵּי eyelids, so called from their volatile motion (die Flatternden), from the root עוּף Pilp. עִפְעֵף (compare Heb. Gramm. § 54, No.4). Job 16:16 Psa 132:4. Poet. עַפְעַפֵּי שַׁחַר eyelids of the dawn, used of the rays of the rising sun, Job 3:9 41:10 . Compare Ἁμέρας βλέφαρον, Soph. Antig. 103, 104. The Arabian poets compare the sun to an eye (in Kamûs العين amongst other things is explained الشمس او سعا عها the sun or its beam), and they ascribe to it eyebrows حواجب الشمس, see Schult. on Job, p. 61.
